name: citeroots
description: CiteRoots is a two-layer measurement framework that complements the MUSES prospective retrieval benchmark by tightening 'any future citation' toward citations that play generative roles in local text (CiteRoots-Rhetoric: passage-level rhetorical role validated against ~1,200 human-gold contexts at Cohen's kappa = 0.896) and citations the citing-paper authors themselves identify as intellectual roots (CiteRoots-Endorsement: 1,518 author-attested generative-inspiration pairs from 753 focal papers, collected through an author-response adjudication workbench). The two layers are empirically separable on the same audit pairs (kappa = 0.896 for rhetorical labeling vs. kappa = 0.037 against author endorsement), establishing that paper-level intellectual debt is not recoverable from local citation rhetoric alone.
citeAs: [Anonymized at submission. Will be filled in at de-anonymization.]
rai:dataCollection: Two collection pipelines feed CiteRoots: (1) Rhetorical layer: each benchmark-aligned focal->cited citation context is classified by a frontier LLM teacher (gpt-5.4-mini with the v6_literature prompt at medium reasoning effort) into a six-category taxonomy, then aggregated to paper-level binary ROOT/non-ROOT labels under a precision-first rule. A distilled Qwen3-8B + LoRA open companion judge is released for scalable inference. ~1,200 contexts were independently human-annotated to validate the teacher (Cohen's kappa = 0.896). (2) Endorsement layer: recent-paper authors were contacted via the workbench and asked which prior works they regarded as generative inspirations for a focal paper of theirs; responses were human-reviewed before entering the released cohort.
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Rhetorical labels: per-context six-way classification, then paper-level binary aggregation under a precision-first rule (positive iff at least one linked context is judged ROOT). Endorsement pairs: human review for review-paper status, self-citation overlap, and consent before inclusion.
rai:annotationsPerExample: Rhetorical layer: one binary ROOT/non-ROOT label per (focal_corpusid, candidate_corpusid) edge. Endorsement layer: one audit verdict per author-attested pair.
rai:annotatorDemographics: Three trained human annotators contributed to the gold audit set. Annotator demographics are anonymized for double-blind review.
rai:dataAnnotationAnalysis: Inter-annotator agreement on the 200-context shared subset: Cohen's kappa = 0.782 (binary tier), 0.715 (six-way). LLM teacher vs. human gold: kappa = 0.896. Distilled Qwen3-8B student vs. teacher: kappa = 0.771.
rai:dataReleaseMaintenancePlan: See MAINTENANCE.md. SemVer-versioned, hosted on HuggingFace + GitHub. Right-to-erasure protocol available indefinitely for endorsement participants.
rai:dataUseCases: Citation-intent classification benchmark. Author-endorsement vs. rhetorical role separability analysis. Training paper-level endorsement classifiers. Tightening retrieval slices on the companion MUSES benchmark.
rai:dataLimitations: Rhetorical layer uses a single canonical judge family (gpt-5.4-mini) plus its distilled student; cross-family judge robustness is documented as a known robustness item. Endorsement cohort is responder-conditioned; absolute endorsement-rate statistics may not generalize to non-responders. Endorsement cohort scale is ~1,500 pairs at v1.0.0; treat as a high-signal calibration slice rather than a full standalone benchmark.
rai:dataBiases: Rhetorical labels are produced by a single LLM judge family; while binary agreement with human gold is high (kappa = 0.896), any judge-family-specific bias propagates into the labels. Endorsement cohort field distribution (Biology, Computer Science, Medicine, Engineering, Chemistry) reflects who responded to the workbench; chi-square test on journal-family distribution detects no strong skew (p = 0.48 at current n).
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: The endorsement layer was collected with explicit author consent (see consent_protocol.md). We redistribute only structured pair-level outcomes after human review: focal_corpusid, candidate_corpusid, audit verdict, eligibility flags, coarse field family. We do NOT redistribute raw author responses, free-text rationales, email addresses, or any other personally identifying information beyond what S2ORC's public author tables already expose via authorid keys.
rai:safetyAndPrivacy: Author consent is documented in consent_protocol.md. Raw narratives and contact information are excluded from the release. A right-to-erasure protocol is available indefinitely for endorsement participants. The rhetorical layer does not contain free-text content; it contains category labels over identifier-keyed citation edges.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Intended use: research evaluation of citation-function classification, author-endorsement modeling, and inspiration retrieval. Not intended for hiring, funding, or career-advancement decisions, nor for adversarial citation generation.

distribution.name: rhetoric_labels_paper_level.parquet
distribution.description: Paper-level binary ROOT/non-ROOT labels for benchmark-aligned focal->cited edges. Aggregated from per-context judgments under a precision-first rule.

distribution.name: human_gold_audit.parquet
distribution.description: Human-gold audit set: ~1,200 context-level annotations with rater agreement metadata. Used to validate the canonical LLM teacher (kappa = 0.896).

distribution.name: endorsement_pairs.parquet
distribution.description: 1,518 author-attested generative-inspiration pairs from 753 focal papers. NO free-text columns. NO author-identifying columns.

distribution.name: endorsement_subsets.json
distribution.description: Manifest defining the 435 context-linked subset and the 402 retrieval-evaluable subset of the endorsement cohort.

distribution.name: cohort_characterization.parquet
distribution.description: Aggregate cohort statistics for the 753 focal papers: field family, year buckets, journal family. No PII.

distribution.name: taxonomy_v7_0.yml
distribution.description: The six-category citation-intent taxonomy used by both the rhetorical labeler and the human annotation workbench.

distribution.name: prompt_v6_literature.txt
distribution.description: Verbatim text of the canonical teacher prompt used by gpt-5.4-mini at medium reasoning effort.

field.name: focal_corpusid
field.description: S2ORC corpusid of the focal (citing) paper.

field.name: candidate_corpusid
field.description: S2ORC corpusid of the cited (candidate) paper.

field.name: label
field.description: Binary aggregated label: ROOT or NON_ROOT.

field.name: focal_corpusid
field.description: S2ORC corpusid of the focal (citing) paper.

field.name: candidate_corpusid
field.description: S2ORC corpusid of the cited (inspiration) paper.

field.name: audit_status
field.description: Verdict from human review: passed, flagged_self_cite, flagged_review_paper, excluded.
